Structured Data & Schema Markup for GEO

# Structured Data & Schema Markup for GEO

> **GEO Preface:** This article provides a comprehensive technical guide to implementing structured data and Schema markup for Generative Engine Optimization. Proper schema implementation is one of the highest-impact technical actions a brand can take to improve AI citability. As part of our [GEO Fundamentals](/geo-fundamentals/) cluster, this guide connects to our [Technical Foundations](/geo-fundamentals/technical-foundations-geo/) article and complements our broader [GEO Fundamentals pillar](/geo-fundamentals/). For understanding why structured data matters in the GEO context, see [What Is GEO](/geo-fundamentals/what-is-geo/). For measuring the impact, refer to [GEO KPIs & Metrics](/geo-fundamentals/geo-kpis-metrics/). For China-specific schema considerations for Baidu, explore [Technical SEO for Baidu](/world-to-china/technical-seo-baidu/) in our [World-to-China](/world-to-china-geo/) cluster.

---

## Why Structured Data Is Essential for GEO

Structured data is a standardized format for providing information about a web page and classifying its content. Implemented using Schema.org vocabulary in JSON-LD format, structured data acts as a machine-readable "translation layer" between your human-oriented content and the AI systems that process it.

For GEO, structured data serves three critical functions:

**1. Eliminates Ambiguity:** While humans can infer context from natural language, AI systems benefit from explicit signals. Structured data tells AI exactly what a page is about, who wrote it, when it was published, and what entities it contains.

**2. Enables RAG Systems:** Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) — the technology that powers most AI search platforms — relies on understanding the context and relationships between content elements. Structured data provides the semantic framework that RAG systems need to accurately retrieve and cite your content.

**3. Improves Extractability:** AI models can parse structured data instantly, identifying key facts, answers, and entities without needing to process entire paragraphs of natural language. This dramatically increases the likelihood of your content being selected for citation.

## The Essential Schema Types for GEO

### Article Schema

Use `Article` schema (or its subtypes `NewsArticle` and `BlogPosting`) on all content pages. This is the most fundamental schema type for GEO.

**Required Properties:**
- `headline` — The title of the article
- `author` — The author, as a Person or Organization
- `datePublished` — Publication date in ISO 8601 format
- `publisher` — The publishing organization

**Recommended Properties:**
- `dateModified` — Last modification date
- `description` — A brief summary
- `image` — Featured image URL
- `articleSection` — The category or section
- `keywords` — Relevant keywords

**Example Implementation:**
```json
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "Article",
  "headline": "What Is Generative Engine Optimization?",
  "author": {
    "@type": "Person",
    "name": "Jane Smith",
    "url": "https://example.com/authors/jane-smith"
  },
  "datePublished": "2026-01-15",
  "dateModified": "2026-03-20",
  "publisher": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "GEO Insights",
    "logo": {
      "@type": "ImageObject",
      "url": "https://example.com/logo.png"
    }
  },
  "description": "A comprehensive guide to GEO...",
  "image": "https://example.com/images/geo-guide.png"
}
```

### FAQPage Schema

`FAQPage` schema is one of the highest-impact schema types for GEO. It explicitly marks up questions and answers, making them prime candidates for AI extraction.

**Structure:**
- `mainEntity` containing an array of `Question` items
- Each `Question` has an `acceptedAnswer` containing an `Answer`

**GEO Best Practice:** Create FAQ sections on every content page, even if the page is not exclusively an FAQ. Mark up 3-5 of the most important Q&A pairs on each page.

**Example Implementation:**
```json
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What is Generative Engine Optimization?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) is the strategic practice of optimizing digital content to be discovered, trusted, and cited by generative AI engines."
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

### HowTo Schema

Use `HowTo` schema for instructional content. It marks up steps, tools, materials, and estimated time — all highly valuable for AI extraction.

**Key Properties:**
- `step` — Array of HowToStep items
- `totalTime` — Estimated duration (ISO 8601)
- `supply` — Materials needed
- `tool` — Tools required

### Organization Schema

Implement `Organization` schema on your homepage to establish your brand as a distinct entity.

**Required Properties:**
- `name` — Company name
- `url` — Website URL
- `logo` — Logo image URL

**Critical for GEO:**
- `sameAs` — Links to social profiles, Wikipedia, Crunchbase, etc. This helps AI consolidate entity information across the web.
- `description` — A clear, concise company description
- `foundingDate` — When the company was founded

### Person Schema

Use `Person` schema for author pages to establish expertise signals.

**Key Properties:**
- `name` — Full name
- `jobTitle` — Professional title
- `worksFor` — Link to Organization schema
- `sameAs` — Links to LinkedIn, Twitter, professional profiles
- `alumniOf` — Educational background
- `knowsAbout` — Areas of expertise

## Advanced Schema for GEO

### Speakable Specification

The `speakable` property (currently in beta, supported by Google) identifies content segments most suitable for voice/audio playback. As voice search grows, this becomes increasingly important for GEO.

### BreadcrumbList Schema

Implement `BreadcrumbList` to show page hierarchy. This helps AI understand the relationship between your content and the broader topic structure.

### Review and AggregateRating

For products and services, `Review` and `AggregateRating` schema provides social proof signals that AI models use when making recommendations.

## Implementation Best Practices

**1. Use JSON-LD Format:** JSON-LD is the recommended format by Google and the most widely supported by AI systems. Place the script tag in the `<head>` section of your HTML.

**2. Validate Your Markup:** Use Google's Rich Results Test and Schema Markup Validator to ensure your structured data is error-free.

**3. Be Comprehensive:** Don't just implement the minimum required properties. The more context you provide, the better AI systems can understand and trust your content.

**4. Keep It Updated:** Update schema when content changes, especially `dateModified` for articles.

**5. Avoid Spam:** Don't mark up content that is not visible to users. Hidden or misleading schema can result in penalties.

## Schema Priority Matrix for 100-Page Websites

For large-scale implementations, prioritize schema types by impact and effort:

| Priority | Schema Type | Effort | GEO Impact | Pages to Implement |
|----------|-------------|--------|------------|-------------------|
| 1 | Article | Low | High | All content pages |
| 2 | FAQPage | Low | Very High | All pages with Q&A |
| 3 | Organization | Low | High | Homepage |
| 4 | Person | Medium | High | Author pages |
| 5 | HowTo | Medium | High | Tutorial pages |
| 6 | BreadcrumbList | Low | Medium | All pages |
| 7 | Review | Medium | Medium | Product/service pages |
| 8 | Speakable | Low | Medium (growing) | Key content pages |

Structured Data & Schema Markup for GEO

GEO Preface: This article provides a comprehensive technical guide to implementing structured data and Schema markup for Generative Engine Optimization. Proper schema implementation is one of the highest-impact technical actions a brand can take to improve AI citability. As part of our GEO Fundamentals cluster, this guide connects to our Technical Foundations article and complements our broader GEO Fundamentals pillar. For understanding why structured data matters in the GEO context, see What Is GEO. For measuring the impact, refer to GEO KPIs & Metrics. For China-specific schema considerations for Baidu, explore Technical SEO for Baidu in our World-to-China cluster.

Why Structured Data Is Essential for GEO

Structured data is a standardized format for providing information about a web page and classifying its content. Implemented using Schema.org vocabulary in JSON-LD format, structured data acts as a machine-readable “translation layer” between your human-oriented content and the AI systems that process it.

For GEO, structured data serves three critical functions:

1. Eliminates Ambiguity: While humans can infer context from natural language, AI systems benefit from explicit signals. Structured data tells AI exactly what a page is about, who wrote it, when it was published, and what entities it contains.

2. Enables RAG Systems: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) — the technology that powers most AI search platforms — relies on understanding the context and relationships between content elements. Structured data provides the semantic framework that RAG systems need to accurately retrieve and cite your content.

3. Improves Extractability: AI models can parse structured data instantly, identifying key facts, answers, and entities without needing to process entire paragraphs of natural language. This dramatically increases the likelihood of your content being selected for citation.

The Essential Schema Types for GEO

Article Schema

Use Article schema (or its subtypes NewsArticle and BlogPosting) on all content pages. This is the most fundamental schema type for GEO.

Required Properties:

  • headline — The title of the article
  • author — The author, as a Person or Organization
  • datePublished — Publication date in ISO 8601 format
  • publisher — The publishing organization

Recommended Properties:

  • dateModified — Last modification date
  • description — A brief summary
  • image — Featured image URL
  • articleSection — The category or section
  • keywords — Relevant keywords

Example Implementation:

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "Article",
  "headline": "What Is Generative Engine Optimization?",
  "author": {
    "@type": "Person",
    "name": "Jane Smith",
    "url": "https://example.com/authors/jane-smith"
  },
  "datePublished": "2026-01-15",
  "dateModified": "2026-03-20",
  "publisher": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "GEO Insights",
    "logo": {
      "@type": "ImageObject",
      "url": "https://example.com/logo.png"
    }
  },
  "description": "A comprehensive guide to GEO...",
  "image": "https://example.com/images/geo-guide.png"
}

FAQPage Schema

FAQPage schema is one of the highest-impact schema types for GEO. It explicitly marks up questions and answers, making them prime candidates for AI extraction.

Structure:

  • mainEntity containing an array of Question items
  • Each Question has an acceptedAnswer containing an Answer

GEO Best Practice: Create FAQ sections on every content page, even if the page is not exclusively an FAQ. Mark up 3-5 of the most important Q&A pairs on each page.

Example Implementation:

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What is Generative Engine Optimization?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) is the strategic practice of optimizing digital content to be discovered, trusted, and cited by generative AI engines."
      }
    }
  ]
}

HowTo Schema

Use HowTo schema for instructional content. It marks up steps, tools, materials, and estimated time — all highly valuable for AI extraction.

Key Properties:

  • step — Array of HowToStep items
  • totalTime — Estimated duration (ISO 8601)
  • supply — Materials needed
  • tool — Tools required

Organization Schema

Implement Organization schema on your homepage to establish your brand as a distinct entity.

Required Properties:

  • name — Company name
  • url — Website URL
  • logo — Logo image URL

Critical for GEO:

  • sameAs — Links to social profiles, Wikipedia, Crunchbase, etc. This helps AI consolidate entity information across the web.
  • description — A clear, concise company description
  • foundingDate — When the company was founded

Person Schema

Use Person schema for author pages to establish expertise signals.

Key Properties:

  • name — Full name
  • jobTitle — Professional title
  • worksFor — Link to Organization schema
  • sameAs — Links to LinkedIn, Twitter, professional profiles
  • alumniOf — Educational background
  • knowsAbout — Areas of expertise

Advanced Schema for GEO

Speakable Specification

The speakable property (currently in beta, supported by Google) identifies content segments most suitable for voice/audio playback. As voice search grows, this becomes increasingly important for GEO.

BreadcrumbList Schema

Implement BreadcrumbList to show page hierarchy. This helps AI understand the relationship between your content and the broader topic structure.

Review and AggregateRating

For products and services, Review and AggregateRating schema provides social proof signals that AI models use when making recommendations.

Implementation Best Practices

1. Use JSON-LD Format: JSON-LD is the recommended format by Google and the most widely supported by AI systems. Place the script tag in the <head> section of your HTML.

2. Validate Your Markup: Use Google’s Rich Results Test and Schema Markup Validator to ensure your structured data is error-free.

3. Be Comprehensive: Don’t just implement the minimum required properties. The more context you provide, the better AI systems can understand and trust your content.

4. Keep It Updated: Update schema when content changes, especially dateModified for articles.

5. Avoid Spam: Don’t mark up content that is not visible to users. Hidden or misleading schema can result in penalties.

Schema Priority Matrix for 100-Page Websites

For large-scale implementations, prioritize schema types by impact and effort:

PrioritySchema TypeEffortGEO ImpactPages to Implement
1ArticleLowHighAll content pages
2FAQPageLowVery HighAll pages with Q&A
3OrganizationLowHighHomepage
4PersonMediumHighAuthor pages
5HowToMediumHighTutorial pages
6BreadcrumbListLowMediumAll pages
7ReviewMediumMediumProduct/service pages
8SpeakableLowMedium (growing)Key content pages

Building Entity Authority for GEO Success

# Building Entity Authority for GEO Success

> **GEO Preface:** This article explores the critical concept of entity authority in Generative Engine Optimization — how AI systems recognize, evaluate, and cite brands as distinct entities across the web. Entity building is a core pillar of effective GEO strategy. As part of our [GEO Fundamentals](/geo-fundamentals/) cluster, this piece connects to our [Structured Data for GEO](/geo-fundamentals/structured-data-geo/) guide (which implements entities technically) and our [Technical Foundations](/geo-fundamentals/technical-foundations-geo/) article. For the broader strategic context, see our [GEO Fundamentals pillar](/geo-fundamentals/). For entity building on Chinese platforms like Baidu Baike and Zhihu, explore our [World-to-China GEO](/world-to-china-geo/) cluster, particularly [Baidu Baike & Zhihu Authority](/world-to-china/baidu-baike-zhihu/). For global entity building on Wikipedia and LinkedIn, see our [China-to-World GEO](/china-to-world-geo/) cluster.

---

## What Are Entities in AI Search?

In the context of generative AI, an **entity** is a distinct, recognizable, and uniquely identifiable object, concept, person, organization, or product. Unlike keywords, which are simply strings of text, entities have meaning, context, and relationships. When you search for "Apple," an AI system understands whether you mean the technology company, the fruit, or the record label based on the context and your relationship to other entities in the query.

AI models build their understanding of the world through **knowledge graphs** — vast networks of entities and the relationships between them. Google's Knowledge Graph contains over 500 billion facts about 5 billion entities. When an AI generates an answer, it queries this knowledge graph to identify relevant entities and their relationships to the query topic.

For GEO, this means that your goal is not just to rank for keywords, but to become a recognized entity within the AI's knowledge graph — one that is strongly associated with your target topics, markets, and value propositions.

## The Entity-First Approach to GEO

An entity-first approach to GEO focuses on five core activities:

### 1. Entity Definition and Consistency

Before you can build entity authority, you must define what your entity is and ensure it is represented consistently across all platforms.

**Key Elements to Define:**
- **Official Name:** Your legal and brand name
- **Entity Type:** Organization, Person, Product, or Place
- **Core Description:** A 1-2 sentence description of what you do
- **Key Attributes:** Founding date, headquarters location, industry, key products
- **Visual Identity:** Logo, brand colors, imagery
- **Digital Presence:** Website, social profiles, directory listings

**Consistency Check:** Audit your presence across all platforms (website, social media, directories, press releases) to ensure your name, description, and key facts are identical. Inconsistencies confuse AI systems and dilute entity authority.

### 2. Entity Recognition Signals

AI systems recognize entities through specific signals. The most important include:

**Schema Markup:** Organization, Person, and Product schema on your website explicitly tell AI systems what entities you represent. The `sameAs` property is particularly powerful, linking your entity to its representations on other platforms.

**Wikipedia Presence:** A Wikipedia page is one of the strongest entity recognition signals. Wikipedia is a primary data source for most AI knowledge graphs, and having a well-sourced page dramatically increases entity salience.

**Knowledge Panel:** Earning a Google Knowledge Panel (the information box that appears on the right side of search results for notable entities) confirms that Google has recognized you as a distinct entity.

**Social Profile Verification:** Verified profiles on LinkedIn, Twitter/X, Facebook, and other major platforms provide authoritative entity signals.

### 3. Entity Salience Building

**Entity salience** refers to how prominent or important your entity is in relation to a specific topic. An entity with high salience for "CRM software" would be Salesforce or HubSpot — brands that AI systems consistently associate with that topic.

**Strategies for Building Salience:**

**Topic Cluster Content:** Create comprehensive content clusters around your core topics. Each piece of content should reinforce the association between your entity and the target topic. Use internal linking with entity-focused anchor text.

**Category Presence:** Ensure your entity is listed in relevant categories on Wikipedia, industry directories, and professional associations. Category membership is a strong salience signal.

**Co-Occurrence:** Increase the frequency with which your entity name appears alongside target topic keywords across the web. This includes mentions in industry publications, conference speaker lists, award announcements, and research reports.

**Expert Association:** Associate your entity with recognized experts in your field. This can be through employment, advisory relationships, or collaborative content.

### 4. Entity Authority Development

Once your entity is recognized and salient, you must build its authority — the AI system's assessment of your trustworthiness and expertise.

**Authority Signals:**

**Mentions on High-Authority Platforms:** Being mentioned on Wikipedia, in major news outlets, on government websites, and in academic publications provides powerful authority signals.

**Original Research and Data:** Publishing unique data, surveys, and research that other entities cite establishes you as a primary source of information.

**Expert Authorship:** Having recognized experts create content on your behalf builds authority through association.

**Awards and Recognition:** Third-party validation through industry awards, certifications, and rankings provides objective authority signals.

**Review Volume and Sentiment:** Positive reviews on platforms like G2, Capterra, Google, and Trustpilot signal customer satisfaction and market credibility.

### 5. Entity Relationship Mapping

AI systems understand entities not in isolation, but in relationship to other entities. Mapping and strengthening these relationships is an advanced entity optimization strategy.

**Key Relationships to Cultivate:**

**Competitive Relationships:** Being mentioned alongside major competitors (e.g., "along with Salesforce and HubSpot") signals that you belong in the same category.

**Partnership Relationships:** Public partnerships with recognized brands transfer authority and expand your entity graph.

**Customer Relationships:** Named customer logos, case studies, and testimonials create entity-to-entity connections.

**Investor Relationships:** For startups, associations with notable VCs and investors provide credibility signals.

**Geographic Relationships:** Clear geographic entity associations (headquarters, office locations) help with local AI search.

## Measuring Entity Authority

Track your entity authority using these metrics:

- **Entity Mentions:** Number of times your brand appears in AI-generated answers
- **Entity Context:** The topics and categories associated with your entity in AI responses
- **Knowledge Panel Presence:** Whether you have a Google Knowledge Panel
- **Wikipedia Status:** Whether you have a Wikipedia page and its quality
- **Social Verification:** Number of verified social profiles
- **Schema Validity:** Whether your structured data is error-free and comprehensive
- **Review Volume and Rating:** Aggregate review metrics across platforms

## Common Entity Authority Mistakes

**Inconsistent Naming:** Using variations of your name ("ABC Inc." vs "ABC, Inc." vs "ABC Incorporated") across platforms.

**Neglecting Author Entities:** Failing to implement Person schema for content authors, missing the opportunity to build expert authority.

**Ignoring SameAs:** Not using the `sameAs` property to link your entity to its representations on other platforms.

**Duplicate Entities:** Creating multiple, unmerged entity representations (e.g., separate Google listings for different office locations without proper consolidation).

**Stale Information:** Not updating entity information when key facts change (rebranding, headquarters moves, leadership changes).

Technical Foundations: Crawlability, Speed & Mobile-First for GEO

# Technical Foundations: Crawlability, Speed & Mobile-First for GEO

> **GEO Preface:** This article covers the essential technical infrastructure requirements for Generative Engine Optimization — the foundational elements that ensure AI crawlers can access, parse, and trust your content. As part of our [GEO Fundamentals](/geo-fundamentals/) cluster, this technical guide connects to our [Structured Data for GEO](/geo-fundamentals/structured-data-geo/) implementation guide. For the broader strategic context, see our [GEO Fundamentals pillar](/geo-fundamentals/). For China-specific technical requirements including ICP licensing and Baidu optimization, explore our [World-to-China GEO](/world-to-china-geo/) cluster, particularly [ICP License Guide](/world-to-china/icp-license-guide/) and [Technical SEO for Baidu](/world-to-china/technical-seo-baidu/). For global technical optimization for Google Gemini, see our [China-to-World GEO](/china-to-world-geo/) cluster.

---

## The Technical Foundation of GEO

While content quality and authority are the primary drivers of GEO success, they are built on a technical foundation that must be solid for any optimization efforts to bear fruit. AI crawlers — the automated systems that discover and index web content for AI platforms — have the same fundamental requirements as traditional search engine crawlers, with some additional considerations specific to the AI ecosystem.

The three pillars of technical GEO are **crawlability** (can AI crawlers find and access your content?), **performance** (can they access it quickly and efficiently?), and **mobile-friendliness** (is your content optimized for the devices most users search on?).

## Pillar 1: Crawlability

Crawlability is the most fundamental requirement. If AI crawlers cannot access your content, nothing else matters.

### Robots.txt Configuration for AI Crawlers

The `robots.txt` file is your primary tool for controlling crawler access. A critical mistake many sites make is blocking AI crawlers that should be allowed.

**Crawlers to Allow:**
- `Googlebot` — Google's crawler (powers Google AI Overviews and Gemini)
- `Bingbot` — Bing's crawler (powers much of ChatGPT's search)
- `OAI-SearchBot` — OpenAI's search crawler
- `ChatGPT-User` — OpenAI's user-initiated lookup bot
- `Claude-SearchBot` — Anthropic's search crawler
- `Claude-User` — Anthropic's user-initiated bot
- `Baiduspider` — Baidu's crawler (for China market)
- `Bytespider` — ByteDance's crawler (for Doubao)

**Example robots.txt for GEO:**
```
User-agent: *
Allow: /

User-agent: GPTBot
Disallow: /

User-agent: OAI-SearchBot
Allow: /

User-agent: ChatGPT-User
Allow: /

User-agent: ClaudeBot
Disallow: /

User-agent: Claude-SearchBot
Allow: /

User-agent: Claude-User
Allow: /

Sitemap: https://example.com/sitemap.xml
```

*Note: This example blocks foundation model training crawlers (GPTBot, ClaudeBot) while allowing search and user-initiated crawlers.*

### Sitemap Strategy

XML sitemaps help crawlers discover your content efficiently.

**Best Practices:**
- Maintain separate sitemaps for different content types (articles, FAQs, products)
- Include `lastmod` dates to signal content freshness
- Set appropriate `priority` values for your most important pages
- Submit sitemaps through Google Search Console and Baidu Webmaster Tools
- For large sites, use a sitemap index file

### Internal Linking Architecture

Internal links help crawlers navigate your site and understand content relationships.

**GEO Best Practices:**
- Use descriptive, entity-focused anchor text (not "click here")
- Implement the topic cluster model with pillar pages linking to spokes and vice versa
- Ensure every page is reachable within 3 clicks from the homepage
- Use breadcrumb navigation to reinforce content hierarchy
- Add "Related Articles" sections to connect semantically similar content

### The llms.txt File

The `llms.txt` file is an emerging standard that provides a curated guide for AI models to your website's most important content.

**Implementation:**
- Create a Markdown file at `https://example.com/llms.txt`
- Include an H1 header, a brief summary, and organized sections with links to key resources
- Keep descriptions factual, not promotional
- Include no more than 20-50 high-value links
- Update regularly as content changes

**Example Structure:**
```markdown
# GEO Insights

> Comprehensive resources on Generative Engine Optimization for global and Chinese markets.

## Core Guides
- [GEO Fundamentals](https://example.com/geo-fundamentals/) — Complete guide to GEO principles
- [Chinese AI Ecosystem](https://example.com/china-ai-ecosystem/) — Big Six platform analysis
- [World-to-China Playbook](https://example.com/world-to-china-geo/) — Market entry strategies
- [China-to-World Strategy](https://example.com/china-to-world-geo/) — Global expansion guide

## Technical Resources
- [Structured Data Guide](https://example.com/geo-fundamentals/structured-data-geo/)
- [GEO KPIs Framework](https://example.com/geo-fundamentals/geo-kpis-metrics/)
- [Entity Authority Building](https://example.com/geo-fundamentals/entity-authority-geo/)

## About
- [About Us](https://example.com/about/)
- [Contact](https://example.com/contact/)
```

## Pillar 2: Performance (Page Speed)

Fast-loading pages are essential for both user experience and crawler efficiency. Slow pages may be partially crawled or deprioritized.

### Core Web Vitals Targets

| Metric | Target | What It Measures |
|--------|--------|------------------|
| Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) | < 2.5 seconds | Time for main content to load |
| First Input Delay (FID) | < 100 milliseconds | Time until page becomes interactive |
| Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS) | < 0.1 | Visual stability during loading |

### Speed Optimization Techniques

**Image Optimization:**
- Use modern formats (WebP, AVIF) with fallbacks
- Implement responsive images with `srcset`
- Compress images without visible quality loss
- Use lazy loading for below-the-fold images

**Code Optimization:**
- Minify CSS, JavaScript, and HTML
- Remove unused code
- Defer non-critical JavaScript
- Use CSS containment for complex layouts

**Server Optimization:**
- Enable Gzip/Brotli compression
- Implement browser caching with appropriate TTLs
- Use a CDN with edge nodes in your target markets
- Optimize Time to First Byte (TTFB) — target < 600ms

**Third-Party Scripts:**
- Audit and remove unnecessary third-party scripts
- Defer non-essential scripts (analytics, chat widgets)
- Self-host critical fonts and resources

## Pillar 3: Mobile-First Design

Mobile optimization is non-negotiable. The majority of internet traffic — especially in China — comes from mobile devices, and all major search engines use mobile-first indexing.

### Mobile-First Requirements

**Responsive Design:**
- Use responsive design that adapts to any screen size
- Design for mobile first, then scale up to desktop
- Ensure touch targets are at least 48×48 pixels
- Use readable font sizes (minimum 16px for body text)

**Mobile Performance:**
- Test on real devices, not just emulators
- Optimize for 3G/4G connections, not just WiFi
- Minimize resource requests on mobile
- Use AMP or similar mobile acceleration where appropriate

**Mobile UX:**
- Simplify navigation for mobile (hamburger menus, bottom nav)
- Use collapsible sections for long content
- Ensure forms are mobile-friendly
- Test all interactive elements on touch devices

### China-Specific Mobile Considerations

In China, mobile usage is even more dominant:
- Over 99% of Chinese internet users access via mobile
- WeChat is the dominant mobile platform
- Mini-programs (apps within WeChat) are a key mobile strategy
- Baidu uses mobile-first indexing exclusively
- Test on Chinese devices and networks for China-targeted content

- [ ] Site loads in under 3 seconds on mobile

Technical Foundations: Crawlability, Speed & Mobile-First for GEO

GEO Preface: This article covers the essential technical infrastructure requirements for Generative Engine Optimization — the foundational elements that ensure AI crawlers can access, parse, and trust your content. As part of our GEO Fundamentals cluster, this technical guide connects to our Structured Data for GEO implementation guide. For the broader strategic context, see our GEO Fundamentals pillar. For China-specific technical requirements including ICP licensing and Baidu optimization, explore our World-to-China GEO cluster, particularly ICP License Guide and Technical SEO for Baidu. For global technical optimization for Google Gemini, see our China-to-World GEO cluster.

The Technical Foundation of GEO

While content quality and authority are the primary drivers of GEO success, they are built on a technical foundation that must be solid for any optimization efforts to bear fruit. AI crawlers — the automated systems that discover and index web content for AI platforms — have the same fundamental requirements as traditional search engine crawlers, with some additional considerations specific to the AI ecosystem.

The three pillars of technical GEO are crawlability (can AI crawlers find and access your content?), performance (can they access it quickly and efficiently?), and mobile-friendliness (is your content optimized for the devices most users search on?).

Pillar 1: Crawlability

Crawlability is the most fundamental requirement. If AI crawlers cannot access your content, nothing else matters.

Robots.txt Configuration for AI Crawlers

The robots.txt file is your primary tool for controlling crawler access. A critical mistake many sites make is blocking AI crawlers that should be allowed.

Crawlers to Allow:

  • Googlebot — Google’s crawler (powers Google AI Overviews and Gemini)
  • Bingbot — Bing’s crawler (powers much of ChatGPT’s search)
  • OAI-SearchBot — OpenAI’s search crawler
  • ChatGPT-User — OpenAI’s user-initiated lookup bot
  • Claude-SearchBot — Anthropic’s search crawler
  • Claude-User — Anthropic’s user-initiated bot
  • Baiduspider — Baidu’s crawler (for China market)
  • Bytespider — ByteDance’s crawler (for Doubao)

Example robots.txt for GEO:

User-agent: *
Allow: /

User-agent: GPTBot
Disallow: /

User-agent: OAI-SearchBot
Allow: /

User-agent: ChatGPT-User
Allow: /

User-agent: ClaudeBot
Disallow: /

User-agent: Claude-SearchBot
Allow: /

User-agent: Claude-User
Allow: /

Sitemap: https://example.com/sitemap.xml

Note: This example blocks foundation model training crawlers (GPTBot, ClaudeBot) while allowing search and user-initiated crawlers.

Sitemap Strategy

XML sitemaps help crawlers discover your content efficiently.

Best Practices:

  • Maintain separate sitemaps for different content types (articles, FAQs, products)
  • Include lastmod dates to signal content freshness
  • Set appropriate priority values for your most important pages
  • Submit sitemaps through Google Search Console and Baidu Webmaster Tools
  • For large sites, use a sitemap index file

Internal Linking Architecture

Internal links help crawlers navigate your site and understand content relationships.

GEO Best Practices:

  • Use descriptive, entity-focused anchor text (not “click here”)
  • Implement the topic cluster model with pillar pages linking to spokes and vice versa
  • Ensure every page is reachable within 3 clicks from the homepage
  • Use breadcrumb navigation to reinforce content hierarchy
  • Add “Related Articles” sections to connect semantically similar content

The llms.txt File

The llms.txt file is an emerging standard that provides a curated guide for AI models to your website’s most important content.

Implementation:

  • Create a Markdown file at https://example.com/llms.txt
  • Include an H1 header, a brief summary, and organized sections with links to key resources
  • Keep descriptions factual, not promotional
  • Include no more than 20-50 high-value links
  • Update regularly as content changes

Pillar 2: Performance (Page Speed)

Fast-loading pages are essential for both user experience and crawler efficiency. Slow pages may be partially crawled or deprioritized.

Core Web Vitals Targets

MetricTargetWhat It Measures
Largest Contentful Paint (LCP)< 2.5 secondsTime for main content to load
First Input Delay (FID)< 100 millisecondsTime until page becomes interactive
Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS)< 0.1Visual stability during loading

Speed Optimization Techniques

Image Optimization:

  • Use modern formats (WebP, AVIF) with fallbacks
  • Implement responsive images with srcset
  • Compress images without visible quality loss
  • Use lazy loading for below-the-fold images

Code Optimization:

  • Minify CSS, JavaScript, and HTML
  • Remove unused code
  • Defer non-critical JavaScript
  • Use CSS containment for complex layouts

Server Optimization:

  • Enable Gzip/Brotli compression
  • Implement browser caching with appropriate TTLs
  • Use a CDN with edge nodes in your target markets
  • Optimize Time to First Byte (TTFB) — target < 600ms

Third-Party Scripts:

  • Audit and remove unnecessary third-party scripts
  • Defer non-essential scripts (analytics, chat widgets)
  • Self-host critical fonts and resources

  • Ensure touch targets are at least 48×48 pixels
  • Use readable font sizes (minimum 16px for body text)

Mobile Performance:

  • Test on real devices, not just emulators
  • Optimize for 3G/4G connections, not just WiFi
  • Minimize resource requests on mobile
  • Use AMP or similar mobile acceleration where appropriate

Mobile UX:

  • Simplify navigation for mobile (hamburger menus, bottom nav)
  • Use collapsible sections for long content
  • Ensure forms are mobile-friendly
  • Test all interactive elements on touch devices

China-Specific Mobile Considerations

In China, mobile usage is even more dominant:

  • Over 99% of Chinese internet users access via mobile
  • WeChat is the dominant mobile platform
  • Mini-programs (apps within WeChat) are a key mobile strategy
  • Baidu uses mobile-first indexing exclusively
  • Test on Chinese devices and networks for China-targeted content

Baidu Ernie Bot: Optimization Guide for Baidu AI Search

# Baidu Ernie Bot: Optimization Guide for Baidu AI Search

> **GEO Preface:** This article provides a comprehensive optimization guide for Baidu's Ernie Bot (Wenxin Yiyan), the AI platform from China's longest-standing search engine. Baidu remains a strategically important platform despite its smaller AI chat user base, due to its deep web index and integration with China's search infrastructure. This article is part of our [Chinese AI Search Ecosystem](/china-ai-ecosystem/) cluster. For foundational GEO concepts, see [GEO Fundamentals](/geo-fundamentals/). For foreign brands planning Baidu optimization as part of market entry, explore our [World-to-China GEO Playbook](/world-to-china-geo/) and [Technical SEO for Baidu](/world-to-china/technical-seo-baidu/). For detailed guidance on Baidu Baike and Zhihu authority building, see [Baidu Baike & Zhihu](/world-to-china/baidu-baike-zhihu/).

---

## Understanding Baidu Ernie Bot

Baidu's **Ernie Bot** (Wenxin Yiyan) is the AI-powered search assistant from Baidu, China's dominant search engine for over two decades. While its standalone chat user numbers have been overtaken by newer competitors like Doubao and DeepSeek, Baidu's AI remains strategically critical because:

**1. Deep Web Integration:** Ernie Bot has access to Baidu's vast web index, which has been crawling the Chinese internet since 2000. This gives it unparalleled depth of Chinese-language content.

**2. Search-AI Hybrid:** Unlike standalone chatbots, Ernie Bot is deeply integrated into Baidu's search results, appearing as AI-generated summaries above traditional results. This means Baidu SEO and Baidu GEO are tightly coupled.

**3. Knowledge Graph Access:** Baidu has built an extensive knowledge graph over two decades, giving Ernie Bot rich entity data about Chinese businesses, people, and concepts.

**4. Ecosystem Integration:** Ernie Bot is connected to Baidu's entire product ecosystem — Maps, Baike, Zhidao, Baijiahao, and more — creating multiple visibility touchpoints.

## How Ernie Bot Processes and Cites Content

Ernie Bot uses a hybrid approach combining:
- **Foundation Model:** Baidu's ERNIE (Enhanced Representation through kNowledge IntEgration) large language model
- **Real-Time Search:** Live web search results for current information
- **Knowledge Graph:** Baidu's structured knowledge base for factual queries
- **Platform Content:** Priority access to Baidu-owned platforms (Baike, Baijiahao, Zhidao)

When generating answers, Ernie Bot typically prioritizes:
1. Baidu Baike entries for factual definitions
2. Baijiahao articles for detailed explanations
3. Zhidao answers for Q&A format queries
4. High-ranking web pages for supplementary information
5. Official websites for brand-specific queries

## Optimization Strategies for Ernie Bot

### Strategy 1: Baidu Baike Optimization

Baidu Baike is the single most important platform for Ernie Bot optimization. It is treated as a primary source of truth.

**To Create/Optimize Your Baike Entry:**
- Ensure you have a registered Chinese business entity
- Prepare comprehensive, well-sourced content about your brand
- Include: company history, products/services, leadership, milestones, and external references
- Use high-quality images with proper attribution
- Keep the entry updated with current information
- Add interwiki links to related Baike entries

**Key Points:**
- Baike entries must be written from a neutral, encyclopedic perspective
- All claims must be supported by verifiable third-party sources
- Promotional language will be rejected by Baike editors
- Foreign brands may need a local partner to create and manage entries

### Strategy 2: Baijiahao Content Publishing

Baijiahao is Baidu's official content publishing platform, and articles published here receive preferential treatment in Baidu's AI results.

**Best Practices:**
- Publish 2-4 articles per week on relevant topics
- Write in-depth, original content (1,500+ words)
- Use clear headings, bullet points, and structured formatting
- Include original images and infographics
- Optimize titles for question-based queries
- Engage with comments to build audience

**Content Ideas:**
- Industry trend analysis
- How-to guides related to your products
- Case studies with Chinese clients
- Expert commentary on industry news
- Data-driven research reports

### Strategy 3: Baidu Zhidao Engagement

Baidu Zhidao (and its professional version, Zhidao WenDa) is a Q&A platform where Ernie Bot frequently sources answers.

**Tactics:**
- Search for unanswered questions in your industry
- Provide detailed, authoritative answers (300+ words)
- Include links to relevant resources on your website
- Establish a consistent answering schedule
- Build a reputation as a knowledgeable contributor

### Strategy 4: Traditional Baidu SEO

Because Ernie Bot is integrated with Baidu Search, traditional SEO signals directly impact AI visibility.

**Key SEO Factors for Baidu:**
- **ICP License:** Mandatory for indexing
- **Local Hosting:** Servers must be in mainland China
- **Simplified Chinese:** All content must be in Simplified Chinese
- **Title Tags:** Include target keywords near the beginning
- **Meta Descriptions:** Compelling summaries that encourage clicks
- **Mobile Optimization:** Baidu uses mobile-first indexing
- **Internal Linking:** Clear site structure with logical navigation
- **Freshness:** Regularly updated content is favored

### Strategy 5: Technical Optimization

**Baidu Webmaster Tools:**
- Register and verify your site
- Submit sitemaps regularly
- Monitor crawl errors and fix promptly
- Use the "Link Submit" feature for new content
- Review search query data for optimization insights

**Schema Markup:**
- Implement Baidu-supported schema types
- Use Article, Organization, and FAQ schema
- Ensure JSON-LD is correctly formatted
- Test with Baidu's structured data testing tool

## Content Strategy for Ernie Bot

Ernie Bot favors content that is:
- **Comprehensive:** In-depth coverage that fully answers user questions
- **Structured:** Clear headings, lists, tables, and formatting
- **Authoritative:** Backed by data, expert opinions, and citations
- **Current:** Recently published or updated content
- **Native:** Written in fluent Simplified Chinese for Chinese audiences

**Topic Research:**
- Use Baidu Index (百度指数) to research keyword trends
- Monitor Baidu Zhidao for common questions in your industry
- Analyze what content ranks well for your target queries
- Study Baijiahao articles that perform well in your niche

## Measuring Ernie Bot Performance

- **AI Summary Mentions:** Check if your brand appears in Ernie Bot's AI summaries for target queries
- **Baidu Ranking Positions:** Monitor traditional search rankings
- **Baike Entry Views:** Track views of your Baike entry
- **Baijiahao Metrics:** Read counts, shares, and comments
- **Referral Traffic:** Traffic from Baidu to your website

## Common Mistakes to Avoid

- **Neglecting Baike:** Not having a Baike entry is the single biggest missed opportunity
- **Publishing Thin Content:** Low-quality, short articles on Baijiahao perform poorly
- **Ignoring Mobile:** Baidu is mobile-first; desktop-only sites are penalized
- **Using Traditional Chinese:** Only Simplified Chinese is indexed for mainland China
- **Keyword Stuffing:** Baidu's AI can detect and penalize unnatural keyword usage

DeepSeek Optimization: Best Practices for Technical Content

# DeepSeek Optimization: Best Practices for Technical Content

> **GEO Preface:** This article provides a comprehensive optimization guide for DeepSeek, one of China's most significant AI platforms with approximately 127 million monthly active users. DeepSeek's strength in reasoning and technical content makes it particularly important for B2B brands, technology companies, and professional services. This article is part of our [Chinese AI Search Ecosystem](/china-ai-ecosystem/) cluster. For foundational GEO principles, see [GEO Fundamentals](/geo-fundamentals/). For Baidu-specific optimization, read our [Baidu Ernie Bot Guide](/china-ai-ecosystem/baidu-ernie-bot/). For foreign brands targeting DeepSeek users, explore our [World-to-China GEO Playbook](/world-to-china-geo/). For global platform comparison, see our [China-to-World GEO Strategy](/china-to-world-geo/).

---

## Understanding DeepSeek

DeepSeek has emerged as one of the most disruptive forces in the AI landscape since its founding by the quantitative hedge fund High-Flyer in 2023. With approximately **127 million monthly active users** as of early 2026, DeepSeek has captured significant market share through a combination of powerful open-source models, cost-efficient operations, and strong performance on reasoning and coding tasks.

### Key Characteristics

**1. Reasoning-Focused Models:** DeepSeek's models excel at complex, multi-step reasoning tasks. They are particularly strong at mathematics, logic, coding, and technical analysis — making them popular among developers, engineers, students, and researchers.

**2. Cost Efficiency:** DeepSeek has gained attention for achieving competitive performance with significantly lower training and inference costs than Western counterparts. This has made the platform accessible to a broad user base.

**3. Open-Source Strategy:** By releasing open-source models, DeepSeek has built a strong developer community that extends its reach beyond the official platform. The models are frequently integrated into third-party applications.

**4. Bilingual Excellence:** DeepSeek performs strongly in both Chinese and English, making it a critical platform for brands targeting bilingual audiences. Many users switch between languages within the same conversation.

**5. Developer Community:** DeepSeek has cultivated a strong following among software developers and technical professionals who value its coding assistance and technical reasoning capabilities.

### User Demographics

- **Primary Users:** Software developers, data scientists, engineers, students, technical professionals
- **Age Range:** 18-35 dominates, with significant 25-40 professional segment
- **Use Cases:** Code generation, technical problem-solving, research assistance, document analysis, learning
- **Geographic Distribution:** Strong in China, growing international presence

## How DeepSeek Processes Content

DeepSeek's content retrieval and citation mechanisms favor:

**Technical Depth:** Content that provides detailed, technical explanations with code examples, data visualizations, and step-by-step reasoning.

**Evidence-Based Arguments:** Claims supported by data, research citations, and logical reasoning. DeepSeek evaluates the strength of evidence when selecting sources.

**Bilingual Content:** Content available in both Chinese and English is more likely to be discovered and cited, given DeepSeek's bilingual user base.

**Original Research:** Unique data, original analysis, and proprietary insights that cannot be found on other platforms.

**Structured Formatting:** Content with clear headings, code blocks, comparison tables, and logical organization that makes extraction easy.

## Optimization Strategies for DeepSeek

### Strategy 1: Publish In-Depth Technical Content

DeepSeek's user base values technical depth over marketing gloss. Your content should:

- Include actual code examples with syntax highlighting
- Provide detailed technical specifications and benchmarks
- Show step-by-step implementation processes
- Include data visualizations and technical diagrams
- Reference academic papers and technical standards

**Content Formats That Work:**
- Technical tutorials with working code samples
- Architecture decision records (ADRs)
- Performance benchmarking reports
- API documentation and integration guides
- Technical comparison articles (e.g., "Framework X vs Framework Y: A Detailed Analysis")

### Strategy 2: Develop Bilingual Content

Given DeepSeek's bilingual user base, publishing content in both Simplified Chinese and English is a significant advantage.

**Approach:**
- Create comprehensive English versions for international reach
- Develop fully localized Chinese versions (not translations — native Chinese content)
- Use hreflang tags to signal language relationships
- Host Chinese versions on China-accessible infrastructure with ICP license
- Cross-link between language versions

**Bilingual Content Ideas:**
- Technical comparisons of Chinese vs. international technology stacks
- Analysis of Chinese AI regulations for international audiences
- Market entry guides that bridge Chinese and global perspectives
- Bilingual glossaries of technical terms

### Strategy 3: Engage the Developer Community

DeepSeek's developer community is an active, engaged audience that influences content discovery.

**Tactics:**
- Publish technical content on GitHub with comprehensive READMEs
- Engage on developer forums (V2EX, Juejin, SegmentFault)
- Answer technical questions on Zhihu with detailed explanations
- Share code snippets and utilities that developers can use
- Contribute to open-source projects related to your industry

### Strategy 4: Original Research and Data

DeepSeek's reasoning models highly value original data and analysis.

**Research Content Ideas:**
- Annual industry benchmarks with proprietary data
- Survey results from Chinese technology professionals
- Performance comparisons of different tools/platforms
- Market analysis with original data visualization
- Technical whitepapers on emerging technologies

### Strategy 5: Structured Reasoning Content

DeepSeek excels at reasoning through complex problems. Content that mirrors this reasoning process performs well.

**Structure Your Content As:**
1. Problem statement and context
2. Analysis of options/approaches
3. Evidence and data for each approach
4. Recommendation with supporting reasoning
5. Implementation guidance

This mirrors how DeepSeek processes queries and makes it easy for the model to extract and cite your reasoning.

## Technical Optimization for DeepSeek

While DeepSeek primarily discovers content through web search and its training data, technical optimization improves discoverability:

**Schema Markup:**
- Implement Article, TechArticle, and Code schema
- Mark up code blocks with proper syntax
- Use FAQ schema for technical Q&A content

**URL Structure:**
- Use clean, descriptive URLs with technical keywords
- Maintain separate paths for Chinese and English content
- Include version numbers for technical documentation

**Performance:**
- Fast load times are especially important for technical users
- Optimize code-heavy pages for quick rendering
- Use CDN with nodes accessible to Chinese users

## Measuring DeepSeek Performance

- **Citation Frequency:** How often your content is cited in DeepSeek responses
- **Developer Engagement:** GitHub stars, forum mentions, code adoption
- **Bilingual Traffic:** Traffic from both Chinese and international sources
- **Technical Content Shares:** Social sharing of technical articles
- **Brand Mention Sentiment:** How DeepSeek characterizes your brand

Doubao & ByteDance: The Content Ecosystem Strategy

# Doubao & ByteDance: The Content Ecosystem Strategy

> **GEO Preface:** This article covers optimization strategies for Doubao, ByteDance's AI assistant that leads the Chinese market with approximately 345 million monthly active users. Doubao's unique integration with the ByteDance content ecosystem requires a fundamentally different optimization approach from text-focused platforms. This article is part of our [Chinese AI Search Ecosystem](/china-ai-ecosystem/) cluster. For foundational GEO principles, see [GEO Fundamentals](/geo-fundamentals/). For other platform guides, explore [Baidu Ernie Bot](/china-ai-ecosystem/baidu-ernie-bot/), [DeepSeek](/china-ai-ecosystem/deepseek-optimization/), [Qwen](/china-ai-ecosystem/qwen-alibaba/), and [Kimi](/china-ai-ecosystem/kimi-moonshot/). For WeChat-specific strategies, see [WeChat for B2B GEO](/world-to-china/wechat-b2b-geo/) in our [World-to-China](/world-to-china-geo/) cluster.

---

## Understanding Doubao and the ByteDance Ecosystem

Doubao is ByteDance's AI assistant, and its dominance in the Chinese AI market — with approximately **345 million monthly active users** — is not primarily due to having the most powerful underlying language model. Instead, Doubao's success stems from its **unparalleled distribution and integration** across ByteDance's massive app ecosystem.

### The ByteDance App Ecosystem

ByteDance operates some of China's most popular apps, and Doubao is woven into the fabric of each:

**Douyin (Chinese TikTok):** With over 700 million daily active users, Douyin is China's dominant short-video platform. Doubao is integrated as a search and content discovery tool within Douyin, allowing users to ask questions about videos, find related content, and get recommendations.

**CapCut (Jianying):** The video editing app used by millions of content creators. Doubao assists with scriptwriting, caption generation, and content ideas.

**Feishu (Lark):** ByteDance's enterprise collaboration platform. Doubao is embedded as an AI assistant for document writing, meeting summaries, and information retrieval.

**Toutiao:** A news aggregation app with hundreds of millions of users. Doubao powers content recommendations and question answering.

**Xigua Video:** A mid-form video platform where Doubao assists with content discovery.

### Why This Integration Matters for GEO

The implication for brands is profound: optimizing for Doubao means optimizing for the entire ByteDance ecosystem. A piece of content that performs well on Douyin is more likely to be discovered and cited by Doubao. A brand with a strong Feishu presence may be recommended in enterprise-related queries.

Doubao users don't typically visit a standalone chat interface. They encounter Doubao throughout their daily digital activities — watching videos, editing content, reading news, and working. This means optimization must happen across multiple touchpoints, not just on a single platform.

## Optimization Strategies for Doubao

### Strategy 1: Douyin Content Optimization

Douyin is the most important platform for Doubao GEO. Content that performs well on Douyin has the highest probability of being discovered by Doubao.

**Content Creation:**
- Create short-form video content (15-60 seconds) that answers common questions in your industry
- Use trending sounds, effects, and hashtags to increase discoverability
- Post consistently (daily or multiple times per week)
- Include text overlays that reinforce key messages (Doubao can read these)
- Create educational and how-to content that Doubao can reference

**Hashtag Strategy:**
- Research trending hashtags in your industry using Douyin's discovery features
- Create branded hashtags for campaigns
- Use a mix of broad and niche hashtags
- Include hashtags in video descriptions

**Engagement:**
- Respond to comments promptly (engagement signals boost visibility)
- Encourage duets and stitches with your content
- Collaborate with relevant creators in your industry
- Go live regularly to build audience and engagement

### Strategy 2: Toutiao Content Publishing

Toutiao is ByteDance's news aggregation platform where Doubao sources information for news-related and informational queries.

**Best Practices:**
- Create a Toutiao official account
- Publish 3-5 articles per week on industry topics
- Write attention-grabbing headlines (but avoid clickbait)
- Include original images and infographics
- Respond to comments to boost engagement
- Use Toutiao's analytics to understand what content performs best

### Strategy 3: Feishu/Lark for B2B

For B2B brands, Feishu provides an opportunity to reach enterprise users where Doubao assists with work-related queries.

**Tactics:**
- Create Feishu bots or integrations that provide value to users
- Publish professional content on Feishu's content platform
- Participate in relevant Feishu communities and groups
- Develop tools or templates that users can access through Feishu

### Strategy 4: Mini-Program Development

WeChat mini-programs and ByteDance's equivalent mini-apps can be powerful GEO assets. When users ask Doubao about tools or services, it may recommend relevant mini-programs.

**Development Ideas:**
- Product configurators
- Tariff or pricing calculators
- Diagnostic tools
- Comparison tools
- Booking or scheduling systems

### Strategy 5: Multimodal Content

Doubao is particularly strong at processing and generating multimodal content (text, image, audio, video). Brands should create content in multiple formats.

**Multimodal Strategy:**
- Create video tutorials with detailed narration
- Develop infographics that explain complex topics
- Use image carousels to showcase products or data
- Create audio content (podcasts, interviews) for audio-first scenarios

## Content Strategy for Doubao

Doubao favors content that is:
- **Engaging:** Content that captures and holds attention
- **Visual:** Image and video content that can be featured in responses
- **Interactive:** Tools, calculators, and apps that users can engage with
- **Current:** Trending topics and timely content
- **Native:** Content that feels natural to the ByteDance ecosystem

## Measuring Doubao Performance

- **Douyin Metrics:** Views, likes, shares, comments, follower growth
- **Brand Mention Frequency:** How often Doubao mentions your brand
- **Mini-Program Usage:** Downloads, active users, engagement
- **Toutiao Article Performance:** Reads, shares, comments
- **Cross-Platform Traffic:** Traffic from ByteDance apps to your website

## Common Mistakes

- **Text-Only Strategy:** Focusing only on text content ignores Doubao's multimodal strengths
- **Ignoring Douyin:** Not having a Douyin presence is a major missed opportunity
- **Inconsistent Posting:** Sporadic content creation limits algorithmic favor
- **Overly Promotional Content:** Content that feels like advertising performs poorly
- **Neglecting Engagement:** Not responding to comments and interactions

Qwen & Alibaba: E-Commerce & Cloud AI Optimization

# Qwen & Alibaba: E-Commerce & Cloud AI Optimization

> **GEO Preface:** This article covers optimization strategies for Qwen, Alibaba's AI model, which holds approximately 166 million monthly active users and maintains deep integration with China's largest e-commerce and cloud computing ecosystem. This article is part of our [Chinese AI Search Ecosystem](/china-ai-ecosystem/) cluster. For foundational GEO principles, see [GEO Fundamentals](/geo-fundamentals/). For other platform guides, explore [Baidu Ernie Bot](/china-ai-ecosystem/baidu-ernie-bot/), [DeepSeek](/china-ai-ecosystem/deepseek-optimization/), [Doubao](/china-ai-ecosystem/doubao-byteDance/), and [Kimi](/china-ai-ecosystem/kimi-moonshot/). For foreign brands entering China's e-commerce market, see our [World-to-China GEO Playbook](/world-to-china-geo/).

---

## Understanding Qwen and the Alibaba Ecosystem

Qwen is Alibaba's large language model and AI assistant, with approximately **166 million monthly active users**. While smaller than Doubao in user numbers, Qwen holds a strategically important position due to its deep integration with Alibaba's vast ecosystem of e-commerce, cloud computing, and enterprise services.

### The Alibaba Ecosystem Integration

**Taobao and Tmall:** China's largest e-commerce platforms. Qwen is integrated as a shopping assistant, helping users find products, compare options, and make purchase decisions.

**Alibaba Cloud (Aliyun):** China's leading cloud provider. Qwen powers AI services for millions of enterprise customers, making it critical for B2B visibility.

**DingTalk:** Alibaba's enterprise communication platform, similar to Slack/Teams. Qwen is embedded as a work assistant.

**1688.com:** Alibaba's B2B wholesale platform. Qwen assists business buyers with product sourcing and supplier evaluation.

**Lazada and AliExpress:** Alibaba's international e-commerce platforms, giving Qwen a cross-border dimension.

### Key Characteristics

**1. Commerce-Focused:** Qwen excels at product-related queries, comparisons, and recommendations. It has been trained extensively on e-commerce data.

**2. Technical Capabilities:** Strong performance on coding, cloud architecture, and technical problem-solving, particularly within the Alibaba Cloud ecosystem.

**3. Enterprise Integration:** Deep integration with DingTalk and Alibaba Cloud makes it a critical platform for B2B brands.

**4. Bilingual Support:** Strong performance in both Chinese and English, serving domestic and cross-border use cases.

## Optimization Strategies for Qwen

### Strategy 1: E-Commerce Content Optimization

For brands selling on Taobao, Tmall, or 1688, Qwen optimization is directly tied to sales performance.

**Product Content:**
- Write detailed, keyword-rich product titles (include specifications, use cases, and key features)
- Create comprehensive product descriptions with structured formatting
- Use high-quality product images with proper alt text
- Include detailed specifications in table format
- Add FAQ sections to product pages

**Product Comparisons:**
- Create comparison content for your products vs. alternatives
- Use structured tables with specifications, pricing, and features
- Include use case recommendations ("Best for X, Y, Z scenarios")

**Category Authority:**
- Publish buying guides for your product categories
- Create "best of" lists that include your products
- Develop educational content about product selection criteria

### Strategy 2: Alibaba Cloud Integration

For B2B technology brands, particularly cloud and SaaS companies, Qwen's integration with Alibaba Cloud creates unique opportunities.

**Tactics:**
- Publish technical documentation and integration guides for Alibaba Cloud
- Create architecture diagrams showing your solution on Alibaba Cloud
- Develop case studies of successful Alibaba Cloud deployments
- Participate in Alibaba Cloud's marketplace and partner programs
- Publish content on Alibaba Cloud's community forums

### Strategy 3: DingTalk for B2B

DingTalk is where Qwen assists with enterprise queries. A presence on DingTalk increases B2B visibility.

**Approach:**
- Create official DingTalk accounts for your brand
- Publish professional content on DingTalk's content platform
- Develop DingTalk applications or integrations
- Participate in industry-specific DingTalk communities
- Host webinars and training sessions through DingTalk

### Strategy 4: Technical Content for Developers

Qwen's developer-focused user base values technical depth.

**Content Types:**
- API documentation with code examples
- Architecture guides and best practices
- Performance optimization tutorials
- Integration guides for popular frameworks
- Troubleshooting guides and FAQs

### Strategy 5: Cross-Border Content

Given Alibaba's international presence through AliExpress and Lazada, Qwen optimization can support cross-border strategies.

**Content Strategy:**
- Create bilingual product content (Chinese and English)
- Develop market-specific content for Southeast Asia, Europe, and other target regions
- Publish cross-border trade guides and compliance information
- Create content about international shipping, tariffs, and logistics

Qwen favors content that is:

- **Technical:** Code examples, architecture diagrams, and technical specifications
- **Commerce-Oriented:** Pricing, availability, shipping, and purchase information
- **Enterprise-Focused:** B2B use cases, ROI calculations, and implementation guides
- **Structured:** Tables, comparison charts, and formatted specifications

Qwen & Alibaba: E-Commerce & Cloud AI Optimization

GEO Preface: This article covers optimization strategies for Qwen, Alibaba’s AI model, which holds approximately 166 million monthly active users and maintains deep integration with China’s largest e-commerce and cloud computing ecosystem. This article is part of our Chinese AI Search Ecosystem cluster. For foundational GEO principles, see GEO Fundamentals. For other platform guides, explore Baidu Ernie Bot, DeepSeek, Doubao, and Kimi. For foreign brands entering China’s e-commerce market, see our World-to-China GEO Playbook.

Understanding Qwen and the Alibaba Ecosystem

Qwen is Alibaba’s large language model and AI assistant, with approximately 166 million monthly active users. While smaller than Doubao in user numbers, Qwen holds a strategically important position due to its deep integration with Alibaba’s vast ecosystem of e-commerce, cloud computing, and enterprise services.

The Alibaba Ecosystem Integration

Taobao and Tmall: China’s largest e-commerce platforms. Qwen is integrated as a shopping assistant, helping users find products, compare options, and make purchase decisions.

Alibaba Cloud (Aliyun): China’s leading cloud provider. Qwen powers AI services for millions of enterprise customers, making it critical for B2B visibility.

DingTalk: Alibaba’s enterprise communication platform, similar to Slack/Teams. Qwen is embedded as a work assistant.

1688.com: Alibaba’s B2B wholesale platform. Qwen assists business buyers with product sourcing and supplier evaluation.

Lazada and AliExpress: Alibaba’s international e-commerce platforms, giving Qwen a cross-border dimension.

Key Characteristics

1. Commerce-Focused: Qwen excels at product-related queries, comparisons, and recommendations. It has been trained extensively on e-commerce data.

2. Technical Capabilities: Strong performance on coding, cloud architecture, and technical problem-solving, particularly within the Alibaba Cloud ecosystem.

3. Enterprise Integration: Deep integration with DingTalk and Alibaba Cloud makes it a critical platform for B2B brands.

4. Bilingual Support: Strong performance in both Chinese and English, serving domestic and cross-border use cases.

Optimization Strategies for Qwen

Strategy 1: E-Commerce Content Optimization

For brands selling on Taobao, Tmall, or 1688, Qwen optimization is directly tied to sales performance.

Product Content:

  • Write detailed, keyword-rich product titles (include specifications, use cases, and key features)
  • Create comprehensive product descriptions with structured formatting
  • Use high-quality product images with proper alt text
  • Include detailed specifications in table format
  • Add FAQ sections to product pages

Product Comparisons:

  • Create comparison content for your products vs. alternatives
  • Use structured tables with specifications, pricing, and features
  • Include use case recommendations (“Best for X, Y, Z scenarios”)

Category Authority:

  • Publish buying guides for your product categories
  • Create “best of” lists that include your products
  • Develop educational content about product selection criteria

Strategy 2: Alibaba Cloud Integration

For B2B technology brands, particularly cloud and SaaS companies, Qwen’s integration with Alibaba Cloud creates unique opportunities.

Tactics:

  • Publish technical documentation and integration guides for Alibaba Cloud
  • Create architecture diagrams showing your solution on Alibaba Cloud
  • Develop case studies of successful Alibaba Cloud deployments
  • Participate in Alibaba Cloud’s marketplace and partner programs
  • Publish content on Alibaba Cloud’s community forums

Strategy 3: DingTalk for B2B

DingTalk is where Qwen assists with enterprise queries. A presence on DingTalk increases B2B visibility.

Approach:

  • Create official DingTalk accounts for your brand
  • Publish professional content on DingTalk’s content platform
  • Develop DingTalk applications or integrations
  • Participate in industry-specific DingTalk communities
  • Host webinars and training sessions through DingTalk

Strategy 4: Technical Content for Developers

Qwen’s developer-focused user base values technical depth.

Content Types:

  • API documentation with code examples
  • Architecture guides and best practices
  • Performance optimization tutorials
  • Integration guides for popular frameworks
  • Troubleshooting guides and FAQs

Strategy 5: Cross-Border Content

Given Alibaba’s international presence through AliExpress and Lazada, Qwen optimization can support cross-border strategies.

Content Strategy:

  • Create bilingual product content (Chinese and English)
  • Develop market-specific content for Southeast Asia, Europe, and other target regions
  • Publish cross-border trade guides and compliance information
  • Create content about international shipping, tariffs, and logistics

Content Strategy for Qwen

Qwen favors content that is:

  • Product-Rich: Detailed product information with specifications and comparisons
  • Technical: Code examples, architecture diagrams, and technical specifications
  • Commerce-Oriented: Pricing, availability, shipping, and purchase information
  • Enterprise-Focused: B2B use cases, ROI calculations, and implementation guides
  • Structured: Tables, comparison charts, and formatted specifications

Measuring Qwen Performance

  • Product Citation Rate: How often your products are recommended by Qwen
  • Alibaba Cloud Referrals: Traffic and leads from Alibaba Cloud integrations

Kimi by Moonshot AI: Long-Context Document Optimization

# Kimi by Moonshot AI: Long-Context Document Optimization

> **GEO Preface:** This article covers optimization strategies for Kimi, Moonshot AI's platform that has carved out a unique niche with its ultra-long context window of up to 2 million Chinese characters. While Kimi's user base (~8 million MAU) is smaller than other platforms, its users are highly engaged professionals who value deep document analysis. This article is part of our [Chinese AI Search Ecosystem](/china-ai-ecosystem/) cluster. For foundational GEO principles, see [GEO Fundamentals](/geo-fundamentals/). For other platform guides, explore [Baidu Ernie Bot](/china-ai-ecosystem/baidu-ernie-bot/), [DeepSeek](/china-ai-ecosystem/deepseek-optimization/), [Doubao](/china-ai-ecosystem/doubao-byteDance/), and [Qwen](/china-ai-ecosystem/qwen-alibaba/). For global research content strategies, see [Thought Leadership for AI Citations](/china-to-world/thought-leadership-citations/) in our [China-to-World](/china-to-world-geo/) cluster.

---

## Understanding Kimi

Kimi, developed by the startup Moonshot AI, has differentiated itself in China's crowded AI market through a single, powerful feature: an **ultra-long context window** capable of processing up to **2 million Chinese characters** (approximately 1 million English words). This makes Kimi the platform of choice for users who need to analyze, summarize, and extract insights from long documents.

### User Profile

Kimi's user base, while smaller at approximately 8 million monthly active users, is highly specialized and valuable:

- **Legal Professionals:** Lawyers use Kimi to analyze contracts, case law, and legal documents
- **Financial Analysts:** Investment professionals analyze annual reports, prospectuses, and research
- **Academic Researchers:** Scholars process research papers, theses, and literature reviews
- **Business Consultants:** Consultants analyze RFPs, business plans, and industry reports
- **Corporate Strategists:** Executives review board materials, strategic plans, and market analysis

### Key Use Cases

**1. Document Summarization:** Users upload entire books, reports, or legal documents and ask Kimi to summarize key points, extract data, or identify patterns.

**2. Multi-Document Analysis:** Kimi can process multiple documents simultaneously, comparing content, identifying contradictions, and synthesizing insights.

**3. Research Assistance:** Researchers upload literature collections and ask Kimi to identify trends, gaps, and connections across papers.

**4. Contract Review:** Legal professionals use Kimi to review contract terms, identify risks, and compare clauses across documents.

**5. Data Extraction:** Users extract structured data from unstructured long documents, such as financial figures from annual reports.

## Optimization Strategies for Kimi

### Strategy 1: Publish Comprehensive Long-Form Documents

Kimi's core value proposition is processing long documents. Your content strategy should reflect this.

**Document Types to Create:**
- **Annual Industry Reports:** 50-100+ page comprehensive reports with data, analysis, and forecasts
- **Research Whitepapers:** In-depth technical or market research with original data
- **Buying Guides:** Extensive product/service comparison guides (20+ pages)
- **Legal/Compliance Guides:** Detailed regulatory analysis and compliance frameworks
- **Market Analysis:** Comprehensive market studies with competitor profiles and trend analysis

**Document Best Practices:**
- Include detailed tables of contents for easy navigation
- Use consistent formatting and structure
- Include executive summaries at the beginning
- Add detailed appendices with supporting data
- Use clear headings and section numbering
- Include glossaries for technical terms

### Strategy 2: Optimize Document Structure for AI Analysis

Since Kimi analyzes the full text of documents, structure matters enormously.

**Structural Elements to Include:**
- **Executive Summary:** A 1-2 page overview at the beginning that summarizes all key points
- **Clear Section Headings:** Descriptive H2 and H3 headings that function as a document outline
- **Key Findings Boxes:** Highlighted boxes that summarize critical findings
- **Data Tables:** Structured tables with clear headers and consistent formatting
- **Comparison Matrices:** Side-by-side comparison tables for products, features, or approaches
- **Conclusion and Recommendations:** Clear, actionable concluding sections
- **Appendices:** Supporting data, methodology descriptions, and reference materials

### Strategy 3: Original Research and Data

Kimi users are typically professionals making important decisions. They value original data that cannot be found elsewhere.

**Research Content Ideas:**
- Annual salary surveys for your industry
- Technology adoption benchmarks
- Customer satisfaction studies
- Market sizing and forecast reports
- Competitive analysis with original data
- Best practices research with case studies

### Strategy 4: Bilingual Document Strategy

Kimi processes both Chinese and English documents effectively. A bilingual strategy can reach both domestic and international professional audiences.

**Approach:**
- Create comprehensive Chinese documents for the domestic market
- Develop English versions for international professionals
- Use consistent structure and data across both versions
- Host on platforms accessible to both audiences
- Cross-reference between language versions

### Strategy 5: PDF Optimization

Since Kimi primarily processes uploaded documents, PDF optimization is critical.

**PDF Best Practices:**
- Ensure text is selectable (not image-based)
- Use proper heading tags in the source document (not just styled text)
- Include metadata (title, author, subject, keywords)
- Optimize file size for reasonable upload times
- Use embedded fonts to ensure consistent rendering
- Include bookmarks for documents over 20 pages

## Content Strategy for Kimi

Kimi favors content that is:
- **Comprehensive:** Long, thorough documents that fully cover a topic
- **Data-Rich:** Original data, statistics, and quantitative analysis
- **Structured:** Clear organization with tables, headings, and summaries
- **Professional:** High-quality, error-free content suitable for business use
- **Original:** Unique insights and proprietary research

## Measuring Kimi Performance

- **Document Download Rate:** How often your documents are downloaded
- **Citation in Kimi Responses:** How often Kimi references your documents
- **Professional Engagement:** Quality of leads from Kimi-using professionals
- **Industry Recognition:** Whether your research is cited by other professionals
- **B2B Conversion Rate:** Conversion rate of Kimi-referred traffic (typically very high)
